summary: Added an Expansion Forge panel that converts realm packs into reusable future-lane blueprints with open slot counts, minigame coverage, gate pressure, asset requirements, and nearest existing-lane references.

queries: dashboard scalability add new project template UX pattern
game level editor slot expansion UI pattern dashboard
design systems extensible component gallery template pattern
pattern_notes: Scalable systems benefit from reusable templates and patterns, not only isolated components.
Component galleries make future construction easier by showing consistent examples, metadata, and variants.
Expansion and editor-style surfaces should expose structured data before asking users to edit raw JSON.

ui_contract: Render a company-level Expansion Forge below Expansion Realms.
Summarize live lanes, wired games, realm packs, and open expansion slots.
Render six reusable lane-family blueprint cards with module id, mechanic, lane count, minigame coverage, gate count, unlock count, and asset checklist.
Blueprint cards link to the nearest existing lane in the matching family for reference.
Blueprints without an existing reference lane render a disabled locked placeholder, not a dead action.
The forge is static and data-derived; it does not create lanes or mutate JSON.

browser.engine: Playwright against local Chrome because the resumed Browser plugin control surface was not exposed
checks: 390x920 viewport: Expansion Forge rendered 6 cards in 1 column with no horizontal overflow, 5 OPEN reference actions, 1 LOCK placeholder, no console errors, and no failed responses.
1280x800 viewport: Expansion Forge rendered 6 cards in 6 columns with no horizontal overflow, summary values 12 lanes live, 12 games wired, 5 realm packs, and 13 open slots.
1280x800 interaction: OPEN on money_source_discovery changed the hash to #lane=money_source_discovery&view=game, activated the game tab, and rendered the lane-specific minigame detail.
1600x900 viewport: Expansion Forge rendered 6 cards in 6 columns with no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.
Inline favicon prevents browser favicon.ico 404 noise while keeping the web app standalone.
